questions.q: The Iron Pillar of Delhi is most famous for which remarkable property?
options: Resisting rust for over a millennium
questions.explanation: The pillar is celebrated for having resisted corrosion for over 1,600 years, a testament to early Indian metallurgy.

questions.q: In which Indian city does the Iron Pillar now stand?
questions.explanation: The Iron Pillar stands in the Qutb complex in Delhi.

questions.q: The Iron Pillar is primarily made of which material?
questions.explanation: The pillar is a column of wrought iron, notable for its purity and resistance to corrosion.

questions.q: Within which famous architectural complex is the Iron Pillar located today?
questions.explanation: The Iron Pillar stands within the Qutb complex near the Qutb Minar in Delhi.

questions.q: To which era is the Iron Pillar generally dated?
questions.explanation: The pillar dates to the Gupta period, around the 4th-5th century CE.

questions.q: In which language and script is the main inscription on the Iron Pillar written?
questions.explanation: The pillar bears a Sanskrit inscription in the Gupta-era Brahmi script.

questions.q: The inscription on the Iron Pillar honours a king bearing which name?
questions.explanation: The Sanskrit inscription praises a powerful king referred to as Chandra.

questions.q: The Iron Pillar is widely regarded as a showcase of early Indian achievement in which field?
questions.explanation: The pillar is a celebrated example of the metallurgical skill of ancient Indian ironsmiths.

questions.q: Approximately how long has the Iron Pillar resisted significant rusting?
questions.explanation: The pillar has resisted corrosion for over a thousand years, drawing worldwide scientific interest.

questions.q: The Iron Pillar is best described as which kind of object?
questions.explanation: The pillar is a tall, free-standing wrought-iron column.

questions.q: The king Chandra named in the inscription is most often identified by historians with which Gupta emperor?
questions.explanation: Many scholars identify the Chandra of the inscription with the Gupta emperor Chandragupta II Vikramaditya.

questions.q: The inscription says the pillar was set up as a standard (dhvaja) in honour of which deity?
questions.explanation: The inscription records the pillar was raised as a standard of the god Vishnu on a hill called Vishnupada.

questions.q: The approximate height of the Iron Pillar above the ground is closest to which figure?
questions.explanation: The pillar stands roughly 7 metres (about 24 feet) tall above its base.

questions.q: The pillar is generally believed to have originally stood at a hill the inscription calls Vishnupada, often located in which region before being moved to Delhi?
questions.explanation: Scholars suggest the pillar originally stood at a Vishnupada hill in central India (commonly linked to Udayagiri) before reaching Delhi.

questions.q: Modern scientific study attributes the pillar's rust resistance largely to which factor?
options: A protective passive film linked to its phosphorus content
questions.explanation: Researchers credit a protective passive layer, formed partly due to the iron's high phosphorus content, for the corrosion resistance.

questions.q: The Qutb complex housing the pillar primarily reflects the architecture of which later medieval power?
questions.explanation: The pillar now stands amid the early Delhi Sultanate architecture of the Qutb complex, though the pillar itself predates it.

questions.q: Which technique was most likely used by ancient smiths to create the large pillar from iron?
options: Forge-welding many smaller lumps of iron
Casting molten iron in a single mould
questions.explanation: The pillar was made by forge-welding together numerous hot lumps of wrought iron, not by casting.

questions.q: The top of the Iron Pillar is shaped to resemble which decorative form?
options: A flat disc with no ornament
questions.explanation: The pillar is crowned by a decorative bell-shaped capital typical of Gupta-era pillars.

questions.q: Which empire's broader cultural and scientific flourishing forms the backdrop for the pillar's creation?
questions.explanation: The pillar belongs to the Gupta era, often called a classical golden age of Indian civilization.

questions.q: The inscription describes king Chandra as having conquered which northwestern region, mentioning the Vahlikas (Bactrians)?
questions.explanation: The inscription credits Chandra with crossing the seven mouths of the Sindhu and defeating the Vahlikas in the northwest.

questions.q: What is the approximate total weight of the Iron Pillar?
questions.explanation: The pillar weighs approximately 6 tonnes, an impressive feat for ancient forge-welded iron.

questions.q: The pillar's deepest section is buried below ground; what feature once anchored it that has since been removed?
options: A bulbous decorative base or supporting framework
questions.explanation: The buried lower portion shows it once had a bulbous base, and a low railing once surrounded it before removal.

questions.q: Which Indian metallurgist of IIT Kanpur led influential modern studies explaining the Iron Pillar's corrosion resistance?
questions.explanation: Metallurgist R. Balasubramaniam of IIT Kanpur published detailed studies attributing the resistance to a phosphorus-rich passive film.

questions.q: Some scholars argue the pillar was relocated to Delhi by which medieval ruler, who is credited with moving notable monuments?
options: A Tomara or early Sultanate ruler
questions.explanation: Tradition links the pillar's move to Delhi with the Tomara king Anangpal or early Sultanate rulers, though details remain debated.

questions.q: The crystalline iron-phosphorus compound credited with forming the protective layer is most associated with which compound name?
options: A crystalline iron hydrogen phosphate hydrate
questions.explanation: Studies describe a thin protective layer of crystalline iron hydrogen phosphate hydrate, enabled by the iron's high phosphorus content.

questions.q: The high phosphorus and low presence of which element, due to ancient charcoal-based smelting, helped the pillar resist rust?
questions.explanation: Charcoal smelting kept sulphur low and phosphorus high, conditions that favoured the protective passive layer.

questions.q: The inscription's reference to king Chandra resting his fame after conquering enemies in battle in the country called Vanga points to which region?
questions.explanation: Vanga in the inscription refers to the Bengal region, where Chandra is said to have defeated a confederacy of foes.

questions.q: A popular but fading local tradition held that touching the pillar in a certain way (e.g. encircling it with one's arms backward) would bring what?
questions.explanation: A folk belief held that encircling the pillar with one's arms behind the back brought good luck; a fence now prevents contact.

questions.q: The Gupta-era identification of Chandra with Chandragupta II is partly supported by comparison with which other source bearing similar royal claims?
options: Gupta gold coinage and inscriptions naming Chandragupta
questions.explanation: Comparisons with Gupta coinage and inscriptions of Chandragupta II strengthen the identification of the inscription's Chandra.

questions.q: A notable dent or damage near the upper portion of the pillar is traditionally attributed to what cause?
questions.explanation: A dent on the pillar is popularly said to have been caused by a cannonball; the story is a local tradition rather than a documented event.
